arkGenesisReleaseDateAndStoreLinks.title: ARK Genesis Ascended Part 1 Release Date, Platforms, and Store Links
arkGenesisReleaseDateAndStoreLinks.intro: Use this section to check the official store status before downloading the Genesis simulation. The DLC is free, but it requires ARK: Survival Ascended as the base game.
arkGenesisReleaseDateAndStoreLinks.subtitle: ARK: Genesis Ascended Part 1 is live as a free DLC for ARK: Survival Ascended. Steam lists July 3, 2026, while the US Xbox and PlayStation store pages list July 2, 2026.

keyFeatures: PS Plus required for online play
Supports up to 70 online players with PS Plus

arkGenesisFreeDlcAndTidesComparison.title: ARK Genesis Ascended Part 1 Free DLC vs Tides of Fortune
arkGenesisFreeDlcAndTidesComparison.subtitle: Genesis Ascended Part 1 is the free ARK: Survival Ascended Genesis content drop. ARK Tides of Fortune is the paid nautical expansion that builds on the Genesis ocean with ships, treasure, naval combat, and new premium systems.
arkGenesisFreeDlcAndTidesComparison.intro: This comparison separates the free Genesis simulation from the paid nautical expansion. Genesis provides the map and core simulation content, while Tides of Fortune adds premium high-seas systems built around ships, treasure, naval combat, Bob cinematics, and new pirate-themed tools.

items.playerTakeaway: Download Genesis first; buy Tides only for the extra nautical expansion content.
Neither product is a standalone game.

items.genesisAscendedPart1: Free Genesis simulation DLC with missions, biomes, creatures, gear, and story-oriented survival progression.
items.arkTidesOfFortune: Paid Bob-focused nautical DLC with ships, naval warfare, treasure hunts, cosmetics, structures, creatures, cinematics, and milestones.
items.playerTakeaway: Genesis is the required content foundation; Tides is the optional premium layer.

items.genesisAscendedPart1: Adds a map-wide ocean biome with overhauled water physics, dynamic waves, realistic buoyancy, islands, hidden fortresses, and wide-scale ocean warfare setup.
items.arkTidesOfFortune: Expands that ocean into shipbuilding, broadside cannon combat, treasure hunting, hostile ship encounters, Ocean Outposts, and pirate-style progression.
items.playerTakeaway: Ocean exploration is in Genesis; ship-heavy pirate gameplay is in Tides.

items.genesisAscendedPart1: HLN-A missions, difficulty levels, glitches, Hexagon rewards, resources, TEK items, and biome survival.
items.arkTidesOfFortune: Ship skill trees for Piracy, Merchant, and Luxury, plus milestones tied to naval fleets, treasure, Genesis gauntlets, and X-Creatures.
items.playerTakeaway: Genesis rewards mission survival; Tides rewards nautical mastery.

items.genesisAscendedPart1: Palaeoctopus, Bloodstalker, Ferox, Megachelon, Magmasaur, and Astrocetus are highlighted on the official store pages.
items.arkTidesOfFortune: Tidepup and Parrot are highlighted as paid DLC creatures.
items.playerTakeaway: Creature collectors get content from both, but Tidepup and Parrot belong to Tides.

items.genesisAscendedPart1: Plasma Claws, Cruise Missiles, Fishing Nets, Mining Drills, Hover Skiff, pressure plates, alarm systems, jump pads, and ocean platforms.
items.arkTidesOfFortune: Hand Cannon, Grapeshot ammo, Shovel, Shipyard, Aquatic Compartment, Cargo Ledger, Market, Bounty Board, Fish Tank, pirate cosmetics, and maritime decor.
items.playerTakeaway: Genesis covers core survival tech; Tides adds ship, treasure, trade, and pirate utility.

items.genesisAscendedPart1: Players who want the free Genesis map, missions, biomes, creatures, and core Ascended ocean overhaul.
items.arkTidesOfFortune: Players who specifically want naval combat, ship customization, treasure loops, Bob cinematics, and premium pirate systems.
items.playerTakeaway: Start free, then upgrade only when the ship systems are worth the price to you.

arkGenesisBeginnerGuide.title: ARK Genesis Ascended Part 1 Beginner Guide
arkGenesisBeginnerGuide.subtitle: Start with HLN-A, use missions to learn the simulation, earn Hexagons, and prepare for each biome before pushing into harder objectives.
arkGenesisBeginnerGuide.intro: Genesis Ascended Part 1 drops you into a fractured virtual simulation where HLN-A guides missions, difficulty choices, rewards, and progression. This starter path focuses only on confirmed systems from the official store pages: missions, glitches, Hexagons, biomes, creatures, gear, and survival preparation.

steps.title: Add the free DLC and launch it through ARK: Survival Ascended
steps.goal: Make sure Genesis Ascended Part 1 is in your platform library before trying to enter the content.
actions: Use the official Steam, Xbox, or PlayStation store page for your platform.
Confirm that ARK: Survival Ascended is installed because Genesis Ascended Part 1 is DLC.
On Steam, use the Download action; on Xbox, use Get; on PlayStation, use Add to Library.
steps.beginnerTip: Do not buy ARK Tides of Fortune thinking it replaces Genesis. Tides is a paid add-on, not the free Genesis map.

steps.title: Treat HLN-A as your first progression hub
steps.goal: Use your holographic companion to understand what the simulation wants from you.
actions: Open HLN-A mission options and review the available mission types.
Use the listed difficulty levels to pick manageable early objectives.
Expect mission variety such as enemy waves, baby creature escorts, boss tracking, and Dino Races.
steps.beginnerTip: Early progress is easier when you use missions as a guided path before exploring high-risk biomes.

steps.title: Start with lower-pressure missions and build reward flow
steps.goal: Use missions to earn rewards before pushing into lethal biomes.
actions: Complete easier mission variants first to learn combat, movement, and survival rules.
Collect mission rewards and use Hexagons on resources, TEK items, and other useful supplies.
Fix glitches while exploring for extra credit and progression value.
steps.beginnerTip: Hexagons are the cleanest early reward loop named by the official pages, so build around them.

steps.title: Prepare by biome
steps.goal: Match your gear and plan to the biome before traveling deeper.
actions: Bog areas emphasize hostile alien swamp survival and canopy threats.
Ocean areas include deep trenches, whirlpools, dynamic waves, buoyancy, islands, and hidden fortresses.
Arctic areas bring frigid peaks and avalanche danger.
Volcanic areas can punish you with fire and eruption hazards.
Lunar areas use alien moon terrain and low-gravity traversal.
steps.beginnerTip: Genesis biomes are designed around strong environmental pressure, so supplies and hazard preparation matter from the start.

steps.title: Prioritize utility from creatures, tools, and movement gear
steps.goal: Use Genesis-specific creatures and equipment to solve movement, survival, and resource problems.
actions: Look for creature roles such as Bloodstalker mobility, Megachelon ocean-base utility, Magmasaur forge utility, and Astrocetus space traversal fantasy.
Use gear progression around Plasma Claws, Cruise Missiles, Fishing Nets, Mining Drills, Hover Skiff, and ocean platforms.
Build interactive structures such as pressure plates, alarm systems, and jump pads when your base plan needs utility.
steps.beginnerTip: The official content list heavily favors mobility, mission rewards, ocean travel, and specialized tools, so build for those systems early.

steps.title: Move from mission learning to biome mastery
steps.goal: Use completed missions and gathered rewards to push into harder zones and tougher objectives.
actions: Return to HLN-A when you are ready for harder mission tiers.
Use earned resources and TEK items to prepare for dangerous biome events.
Expand into ocean, volcanic, arctic, bog, and lunar routes once your gear and tame plan can handle the hazard profile.
steps.beginnerTip: The simulation rewards preparation, mission progress, and careful biome routing.

arkGenesisMapBiomesAndOceanOverhaul.title: ARK Genesis Ascended Part 1 Map Biomes and Ocean Overhaul
arkGenesisMapBiomesAndOceanOverhaul.subtitle: Genesis Ascended Part 1 centers on lethal simulation biomes: Bog, Ocean, Arctic, Volcanic, and Lunar. Ascended expands the ocean into a map-wide maritime space with new water physics, waves, buoyancy, islands, and hidden fortresses.
arkGenesisMapBiomesAndOceanOverhaul.intro: Use this biome grid as a front-page route planner for where to go, what to expect, and which official biome features matter most before entering each part of the simulation.

items.officialDescription: Fetid alien bogs and treacherous canopy survival.
items.routeNote: Use the Bog card for players searching swamp survival, canopy-heavy traversal, and Bloodstalker-focused routing.

items.officialDescription: Deep undersea trenches expanded in Ascended into a massive map-wide ocean biome.
items.routeNote: Use the Ocean card for searches around ocean biome, sea travel, mobile ocean bases, hidden fortresses, and the new Ascended water system.

items.officialDescription: Frigid mountain peaks inside the Genesis simulation.
items.routeNote: Use the Arctic card for players planning cold-region routes, mountain travel, and Ferox-focused preparation.

items.officialDescription: Dangerous volcanic hellscapes with eruption-level punishment.
items.routeNote: Use the Volcanic card for players searching fire hazards, volcanic exploration, and Magmasaur-related progression.

items.officialDescription: Wild low-gravity surface of an alien moon.
items.routeNote: Use the Lunar card for players searching moon biome, low gravity, alien terrain, and Astrocetus-related exploration.

items.officialDescription: A new map-wide ocean layer built on an overhauled water physics system.
items.routeNote: Use this featured card to connect Genesis ocean searches with shipbuilding, naval combat, treasure hunts, and Ocean Outpost interest from Tides of Fortune.

arkGenesisMissionsHexagonsAndGlitches.title: ARK Genesis Ascended Part 1 Missions, Hexagons, and Glitches
arkGenesisMissionsHexagonsAndGlitches.subtitle: Track the main Genesis Part 1 progression loop: HLN-A mission tests, world glitches, Hexagon rewards, and exchange priorities.
arkGenesisMissionsHexagonsAndGlitches.intro: Genesis Ascended Part 1 uses HLN-A as the mission guide inside a fractured simulation. Players progress by clearing missions across different difficulty levels, fixing scattered glitches for extra credit, and spending rewarded Hexagons on resources, TEK items, and other useful supplies.

items.objective: Choose from a wide array of simulation missions offered by HLN-A.
items.rewardLoop: Earn Hexagons through mission completion and use them for progression supplies.

items.objective: Gun down waves of enemies with the aid of mission power-ups.
items.rewardLoop: Combat-focused missions feed the Hexagon reward economy.

items.objective: Protect vulnerable baby creatures during escort-style mission routes.
items.difficulty: Variable by route and threat level
items.rewardLoop: Successful escorts contribute to the mission reward pool.

items.objective: Track powerful bosses through the simulation before the final confrontation.
items.rewardLoop: Boss-track progress supports Genesis story and mission completion goals.

items.objective: Compete in high-speed Dino Race missions built around movement and route control.
items.rewardLoop: Race completions add another repeatable path into Hexagon earnings.

items.objective: Fix glitches scattered throughout the simulation for extra credit.
items.rewardLoop: Glitch cleanup adds extra progress beyond standard mission runs.

items.objective: Spend rewarded Hexagons after missions and glitch cleanup.
items.rewardLoop: Hexagons can be spent on resources, TEK items, and other useful supplies.

arkGenesisGearWeaponsVehiclesAndStructur.title: ARK Genesis Ascended Part 1 Gear, Weapons, Vehicles, and Structures
arkGenesisGearWeaponsVehiclesAndStructur.subtitle: Use this grid to plan what to craft first: combat weapons, resource tools, vehicle tech, and interactive base structures.
arkGenesisGearWeaponsVehiclesAndStructur.intro: Genesis Ascended Part 1 expands the crafting loop with combat gear, survival tools, advanced vehicles, and interactive structures. The most searched items are the Plasma Claws, Cruise Missiles, Fishing Nets, Mining Drills, Hover Skiff, and ocean-building pieces.

arkGenesisPatchNotesServersAndSystemRequ.title: ARK Genesis Ascended Part 1 Patch Notes, Servers, and System Requirements
arkGenesisPatchNotesServersAndSystemRequ.subtitle: A bottom-of-page update tracker for Genesis Ascended Part 1 launch changes, platform versions, server status, and install requirements.
arkGenesisPatchNotesServersAndSystemRequ.intro: The launch patch for Genesis Ascended Part 1 updated legacy Genesis content for Ascended, added the new creature lineup, overhauled the Ocean biome, and added multi-layer map support. PC players should also check the 180 GB storage requirement and SSD requirement before installing, because apparently survival begins before the download even finishes.

items.playerImpact: Updated all legacy Genesis creatures, items, structures, and environments for Genesis Ascended Part 1.
Xbox client version aligned with the Genesis Part 1 launch patch.
PlayStation client version aligned with the Genesis Part 1 launch patch.
Added Palaeoctopus, X-Acro, X-Deinosuchus, X-Archelon, X-Xiphactinus, and X-Cryolophosaurus.
Astrocetus is listed as a new breedable creature in the launch patch.
Genesis Part 1 received a massive Ocean biome overhaul with the Ascended release.
Added support for multi-layer maps across the various Map UIs.
Added Dreadlocks, Viking, Romantic, and Ponytail with Bangs hairstyle and facial hair options.
Windows 10/11, AMD Ryzen 5 2600X or Intel Core i7-6800K, 16 GB RAM, AMD Radeon RX 5600 XT or NVIDIA GeForce 1080, DirectX 12, broadband internet, 180 GB available space, SSD required.
Windows 10/11, AMD Ryzen 5 3600X or Intel i5-10600K, 32 GB RAM, AMD Radeon RX 6800 or NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3080, DirectX 12, broadband internet, 180 GB available space, SSD required.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about ARK Genesis Ascended Part 1

questions.question: Is ARK Genesis Ascended Part 1 free?
questions.answer: Yes. ARK Genesis Ascended Part 1 is a free DLC for ARK: Survival Ascended on Steam, PS5, and Xbox Series X|S. You only need the base game to play it.

questions.question: Does ARK Genesis Ascended Part 1 require ARK: Survival Ascended?
questions.answer: Yes. ARK Genesis Ascended Part 1 is DLC, not a standalone game, so ARK: Survival Ascended must be installed and owned on the same platform before you can enter the Genesis simulation.

questions.question: What is the difference between ARK Genesis Ascended Part 1 and ARK Tides of Fortune?
questions.answer: ARK Genesis Ascended Part 1 is the free Genesis map, missions, biomes, and creatures. ARK Tides of Fortune is a separate paid nautical expansion that adds ships, naval combat, treasure hunts, and pirate systems on top of the Genesis ocean.

questions.question: When did ARK Genesis Ascended Part 1 release?
questions.answer: ARK Genesis Ascended Part 1 went live in early July 2026. Steam lists July 3, 2026, while the US Xbox and PlayStation store pages list July 2, 2026.

questions.question: Which biomes are in ARK Genesis Ascended Part 1?
questions.answer: ARK Genesis Ascended Part 1 includes five core simulation biomes: Bog, Ocean, Arctic, Volcanic, and Lunar. Ascended also adds a map-wide ocean overhaul with new water physics, waves, buoyancy, islands, and hidden fortresses.

questions.question: Where can I download ARK Genesis Ascended Part 1?
questions.answer: Download ARK Genesis Ascended Part 1 from the official Steam, Xbox Store, or PlayStation Store page for your platform. Use Download on Steam, Get on Xbox, or Add to Library on PlayStation.
